04 May 2026 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

    Other Operations

  • Lethal Kinetic Strike, May 4, 2026 SOUTHCOM 04 May 2026 -- On May 4, at the direction of the commander of U.S. Southern Command, Gen. Francis L. Donovan, Joint Task Force Southern Spear conducted a lethal kinetic strike on a vessel operated by Designated Terrorist Organizations.

    • Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • Situation In Hormuz Still 'Critical' Says Shipping Security Monitor RFE/RL 04 May 2026 -- The United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO) shipping security monitor has issued a Joint Maritime Information Center advisory saying conditions for transiting the Strait of Hormuz remain "critical" amid ongoing regional military operations, with increased naval presence, congestion, and vessels likely to be hailed over VHF radio by security forces in the region.

  • CENTCOM Head Says Iran Launched 'Cruise Missiles, Drones, And Small Boats' At US Navy RFE/RL 04 May 2026 -- US forces intercepted cruise missiles and drones fired by Tehran on May 4, a US admiral said, as Washington launched an operation to guide stranded "neutral" ships out of restricted waters near the Strait of Hormuz.
  • US Says 6 Iranian Boats Destroyed Amid Effort To Restart Ship Traffic; Iran Fires Missiles At UAE RFE/RL 04 May 2026 -- US military forces said they destroyed six ⁠Iranian small ⁠boats and intercepted Iranian missiles and drones as a new American effort to get commercial shipping traffic flowing through the critical Strait of Hormuz got underway.

  • Iran Outlines New 'Control Area' In Strait of Hormuz RFE/RL 04 May 2026 -- Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps (IRGC) has warned that maritime activity in the Strait of Hormuz must comply with its naval protocols or face enforcement action.
  • May 4: Iran repels US push at Strait of Hormuz, hits warships after they ignore warnings Press TV 04 May 2026 -- Iran consolidated its unchallenged control and authority over the Strait of Hormuz on Monday amid a widening collapse of US regional strategy, pushing back decisively against Washington's latest military offensives.
  • No commercial ship or oil tanker transited Strait of Hormuz in past hours: IRGC Press TV 04 May 2026 -- No commercial vessels or oil tankers have transited the Strait of Hormuz in the past several hours, the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) said Monday, dismissing recent US claims as "baseless and outright false."
  • Iran Navy confirms it fired missiles, drones to warn US warships approaching Hormuz Strait Press TV 04 May 2026 -- The Iranian Navy has fired missiles and drones to warn US warships approaching the Strait of Hormuz in an attempt to challenge Iran's control over the key waterway in the Persian Gulf.
  • IRGC vows to 'forcefully stop' ships violating Strait of Hormuz regulations Press TV 04 May 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has vowed to "forcefully stop" vessels attempting to defy the management rules enforced by its Navy regarding international transit through the strategic Strait of Hormuz.
  • Crew of US-seized Iranian vessel transferred to Pakistan for repatriation: Report Press TV 04 May 2026 -- The crew from the Iranian commercial vessel Touska that has been seized by the US as part of Washington's continued illegal naval blockade against the Islamic Republic have reportedly been transferred to Pakistan in preparation for their return to Iran.
  • Iran's military vows to target any foreign forces approaching Strait of Hormuz Press TV 04 May 2026 -- The commander of Iran's highest operational command unit has warned that the Iranian Armed Forces will target any foreign forces, particularly from the US military, that attempt to approach or enter the strategic Strait of Hormuz.

  • Tehran Calls On US To Move Away From 'Excessive Demands' RFE/RL 04 May 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmail Baqaei has called on the United States to back away from its "excessive demands" to allow peace negotiations between the two countries to move forward.
  • Events in Strait of Hormuz prove there's no military solution to political crisis: Iranian FM Press TV 04 May 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi has stated that recent developments in the Strait of Hormuz have once again made it crystal clear that there is no military solution to the political crisis in the West Asian region, reaffirming the Islamic Republic's policy of pursuing diplomacy from a position of strength.
  • Iranian defense official: Enemy's cost will exceed tolerance threshold Press TV 04 May 2026 -- A senior Iranian defense official has warned that any hostile move by the US and Israeli enemy against the Islamic Republic will exact a price far beyond their threshold of tolerance.
  • Persian Gulf states must stop 'borrowing security' from extra-regional powers: Baghaei Press TV 04 May 2026 -- Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baghaei has called on the Persian Gulf states to stop trying to borrow security from extra-regional powers, emphasizing that the US military presence in the area serves only as a source of instability and endangers the very countries hosting those bases.
  • US claim of sinking Iranian boats 'a lie', senior military official tells IRIB Press TV 04 May 2026 -- A senior Iranian military official has rejected a claim by the United States military that it has sunk several Iranian boats as part of an attempt to open the Strait of Hormuz, the IRIB News reports.
  • No nuclear talks on agenda with US, Araghchi tells lawmakers Press TV 04 May 2026 -- A senior Iranian lawmaker has quoted Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi as saying that the Islamic Republic is not currently engaged in negotiations with the enemy on the nuclear issue.
  • After receiving response, Iran says 'endless US greed' knows no bounds Press TV 04 May 2026 -- Iran says the United States has not abandoned its pattern of excessive demands and endless greed, confirming the delivery of Washington's response to Tehran's proposed 14-point plan regarding mutual negotiations meant to end the war against the Islamic Republic.
  • Iran says U.S. has responded to Tehran's 14-point plan to end war People's Daily 04 May 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baghaei said on Sunday the United States has responded to Iran's 14-point proposed plan to end the war.

  • Uncertainty continues over safety in the Strait of Hormuz UN News 04 May 2026 -- Amid claims and counter-claims of strikes and confrontations in the crucial Strait of Hormuz between Iran and the United States, UN maritime officials continue to urge vessels to exercise "maximum caution".

  • Pakistan, Iran FMs discuss regional situation, stress diplomacy for peace People's Daily 04 May 2026 -- Pakistan's Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Mohammad Ishaq Dar held a telephone conversation with his Iranian counterpart Seyed Abbas Araghchi on Sunday night, focusing on the evolving regional situation and efforts for peace, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Pakistan said on Monday.
  • Pakistan Facilitates Transfer of Iranian Crew Members Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 04 May 2026 -- As a confidence-building measure by the United States of America, twenty-two crew members held aboard the seized Iranian container ship, 'MV Touska', have been evacuated to Pakistan.
  • Pakistan facilitates transfer of Iranian crew members from vessel seized by U.S. People's Daily 04 May 2026 -- Pakistan has facilitated the transfer of 22 Iranian crew members who had been held aboard a container ship seized by the United States last month, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs said on Monday.

  • Aboul Gheit condemns Iranian attacks on the UAE League of Arab States 04 May 2026 -- Mr. Ahmed Aboul Gheit, Secretary-General of the League of Arab States, strongly condemned the renewed treacherous Iranian aggression against the United Arab Emirates, carried out through armed drones and missiles targeting civilian facilities, in flagrant violation of international law and the ceasefire agreement.
  • HE the GCCSG Strongly Condemns the Targeting of an Emirati Tanker Belonging to ADNOC While Passing Through the Strait of Hormuz Gulf Cooperation Council 04 May 2026
  • HE GCCSG: Flagrant Iranian Attacks on the UAE are Dangerous Act of Aggression & Blatant Escalation Threatening Regional Security & Stability Gulf Cooperation Council 04 May 2026
  • UAE Slams 'Unacceptable' Drone And Missile Attacks RFE/RL 04 May 2026 -- The United Arab Emirates said that it had come under Iranian missile and drone attack, strongly condemning the strikes as a "dangerous escalation" and holding Tehran fully responsible for their consequences.
  • HRH the Crown Prince Phones UAE President, Reaffirms Saudi Support Against Iranian Attacks SPA 04 May 2026 -- His Royal Highness Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud, Crown Prince and Prime Minister, made a phone call today to President of the United Arab Emirates Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan, during which he expressed the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia's strong condemnation and denunciation of the unjustified Iranian attacks targeting the United Arab Emirates. He reaffirmed the Kingdom's support for the UAE in its defense of its security and stability

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Army convenes industry leaders for AI tabletop exercise focused on cyber defense US Army 04 May 2026 -- On April 27, the Army convened 14 senior cybersecurity executives from leading technology companies at the Pentagon for the second iteration of its artificial intelligence tabletop exercise, an effort designed to accelerate adoption of agentic AI for cyber defense.
  • Training Command Updates Reconnaissance Training USMC News 04 May 2026 -- Training Command launched Ground Reconnaissance Course as part of an updated Marine Reconnaissance training curriculum at Marine Corps Base Camp Pendleton, April 27. GRC is now the initial course in the updated curriculum, which replaces Basic Reconnaissance Course with two new courses: GRC and Amphibious Reconnaissance Course. This change comes from analysis of reconnaissance training and support to Force Design initiatives, including Scout Platoons and multidomain operations.
  • 944th Fighter Wing sharpens readiness during Desert Hammer 26-2 US Air Force 04 May 2026 -- Airmen from the 944th Fighter Wing traveled north from Luke Air Force Base to Camp Navajo May 1-3 for Desert Hammer 26-2, the latest evolution of the wing's ongoing readiness training designed to build battlefield skills, test mission-essential tasks and prepare Citizen Airmen for operations beyond home station.
  • Air Force greenlights T-7A Red Hawk for production following Milestone C US Air Force 04 May 2026 -- The T-7A Red Hawk has officially been cleared for production. Following Milestone C approval on April 23, the Air Force is moving forward with a $219 million contract for the first 14 advanced trainer aircraft along with associated spares, support equipment, and training from Boeing Defense, Space & Security.
  • FRCE reaches milestone with Global TransPark lease agreement US Navy 04 May 2026 -- A pioneering lease agreement between the Navy and North Carolina will provide enhanced aviation readiness for the nation's warfighters, while leaders anticipate the facility will bring growth to eastern North Carolina in the form of jobs and economic impact.
  • Argentine and U.S. Navies Conduct Bilateral Maritime Engagement in Atlantic Ocean US Navy 04 May 2026 -- The Argentine and U.S. navies conducted a bilateral maritime engagement as part of U.S. Naval Forces Southern Command (USNAVSOUTH)/U.S. 4th Fleet's Southern Seas 2026 deployment in the Atlantic Ocean, April 28-May 1.
  • Texas Returns From Deployment US Navy 04 May 2026 -- The Virginia-class fast-attack submarine USS Texas (SSN 775), under the command of Cmdr. Andrew S. McGovern, returned to Naval Submarine Base New London Friday, May 1, completing a six-month deployment to U.S. Southern Command area of responsibility.
  • U.S. Naval Forces Southern Command/U.S. 4th Fleet Completes FLEX in Key West US Navy 04 May 2026 -- U.S. Naval Forces Southern Command / U.S. 4th Fleet (USNAVSOUTH/4th Fleet) successfully concluded its annual Fleet Experimentation (FLEX) event from April 24-30.
  • New deep space radar capability employed early United States Space Force 04 May 2026 -- To counter growing threats from the proliferation of man-made objects and potential adversary activities on orbit, the U.S. Space Force has fast-tracked the employment of the Deep Space Advanced Radar Capability (DARC), a system designated ST-25.
  • DESRON 7, Task Force Ashland complete Balikatan 2026 Multinational Maritime Event U.S. 7th Fleet 04 May 2026 -- Members of Destroyer Squadron (DESRON) 7 and Task Force Ashland, composed of Whidbey Island-class amphibious dock landing ship USS Ashland (LSD 48) and embarked Marines from the I Marine Expeditionary Force, completed the Multilateral Maritime Event portion of Exercise Balikatan 2026, May 1.
  • Argentine and U.S. Navies Conduct Bilateral Maritime Engagement in Atlantic Ocean SOUTHCOM 04 May 2026 -- The Argentine and U.S. navies conducted a bilateral maritime engagement as part of U.S. Naval Forces Southern Command (USNAVSOUTH)/U.S. 4th Fleet's Southern Seas 2026 deployment in the Atlantic Ocean, April 28-May 1.

  • NATO Allies test layered counter-drone defences in Romania in support of Eastern Sentry NATO Allied Air Command 04 May 2026 -- NATO Allies tested layered counter-drone defences in Romania this month to accelerate Integrated Air and Missile Defence (IAMD) integration, turning emerging technology into interoperable capability under realistic conditions.
  • NATO Secretary General attends the European Political Community Summit in Armenia NATO 04 May 2026 -- On Monday (4 May 2026), NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte joined European leaders and partners for the European Political Community Summit in Yerevan to discuss shared security challenges.

  • Russia condemns NATO for significantly expanding its military activities in Baltic region Press TV 04 May 2026 -- Russia has condemned NATO for significantly expanding its military operations in the Baltic Sea, particularly near the Kaliningrad region, warning that the alliance's growing presence poses risks to regional stability and international navigation.

  • President of Ukraine and NATO Secretary General Discussed Defense Support for Ukraine and Attracting New Contributions to PURL President of Ukraine 04 May 2026
  • Defense AI Center A1: Artificial intelligence will accelerate and enhance decision-making on the battlefield Ukraine MOD 04 May 2026 -- Artificial intelligence will provide Ukraine with a battlefield advantage by increasing response speed. It will enable Ukraine to outpace the enemy through faster data processing and decision-making across the full spectrum of operations — from strategic planning to tactical execution on the battlefield.

  • Enhanced Defence and Security Cooperation with Japan Australia DOD 04 May 2026 -- Australia and Japan have agreed next steps to enhance defence and security cooperation, in order to support our shared ambition for a peaceful, stable and prosperous region.
  • Enhanced defence and security cooperation with Japan Prime Minister of Australia 04 May 2026
  • Leaders Statement on Enhanced Defence and Security Cooperation Australia - Department of the Prime Minister and Cabinet 04 May 2026
  • Project of Concern Summit on Offshore Patrol Vessel project Australia DOD 04 May 2026 -- This week, Defence convened A Project of Concern Summit in Canberra in relation to the Arafura class Offshore Patrol Vessel (OPV) project.
  • Autonomous aircraft boost logistics reach Australia DOD 04 May 2026 -- Air Force's Jericho Disruptive Innovation (JDI) team is making headway on autonomous aerial logistics systems to support agile operations and foster national resilience in Defence capacity.
  • Partners' combined air defence focus Australia DOD 04 May 2026 -- The challenge of combining air defence systems to extend coverage and build layered protection brought partner nations together during Exercise Balikatan.
  • A delicate dance in the deep Australia DOD 04 May 2026 -- As a four-ship multinational task group conducted a replenishment at sea (RAS), the surface was alive with coordinated movement.

  • Berlin downplays US plan to cut troop levels in Germany Press TV 04 May 2026 -- Germany's Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul has downplayed the impact of the US decision to withdraw troops from Germany and Washington's plans to suspend the deployment of long-range Tomahawk cruise missiles in the country, insisting the moves will not weaken NATO's deterrence in Europe.

  • Indonesia, Japan hold follow-up defense ministers' meeting Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 04 May 2026 -- Indonesian Defense Minister Sjafrie Sjamsoeddin and Japanese Defense Minister Shinjiro Koizumi arrived in Jakarta on Monday for a follow-up defense ministers' meeting. The meeting follows their previous talks held in Bali on Sunday, according to Antara.

  • The Estonian Defence Forces exercise Spring Storm 2026 has begun Estonia Defence Forces 04 May 2026 -- On Monday, May 4, the military exercise Spring Storm 2026 began. The exercise will last for nearly a month and, at its peak, will involve over 12,000 soldiers from Estonia and allied nations.

  • Chairman Mast Introduces Bill to Ensure Foreign Military Financing Puts America First U.S. House of Representatives Committee on Foreign Affairs 04 May 2026 -- Today, House Foreign Affairs Committee Chairman Brian Mast introduced the Foreign Military Financing Loan Authorization Act of 2026 to give the State Department the green light to arm U.S. partners and allies on terms that put America first.

  • Rubio Heads To Rome, Vatican As Iran War Strains US Ties With Europe RFE/RL 04 May 2026 -- US Secretary of State Marco Rubio is set to visit Rome and the Vatican on May 6-8 in what officials describe as an effort to shore up strained ties with key European partners as the war in Iran deepens divisions across the Atlantic.

  • United States Welcomes Ireland's Signing of the Artemis Accords US Dept. of State 04 May 2026 -- The Department of State congratulates Ireland on joining the Artemis Accords. Ireland is the 66th country to sign the Accords to date, pledging its commitment to peaceful space exploration and use.
  • United States Welcomes Malta's Signing of the Artemis Accords US Dept. of State 04 May 2026 -- The Department of State congratulates the Republic of Malta on joining the Artemis Accords. Malta is the 65th country to sign the Accords to date, pledging its commitment to peaceful space exploration and use.

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 4 May 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 04 May 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots | Roaring Lion (Lebanon)

  • Hezbollah targets elite Israeli infantry brigade, casualties inflicted Press TV 04 May 2026 -- Fighters from the Hezbollah resistance movement have carried out a series of operations against Israeli military forces in southern Lebanon, causing several casualties.
  • Berri: No talks with Israel until attacks on Lebanon stop Press TV 04 May 2026 -- Lebanon's Parliament Speaker Nabih Berri has stressed that no talks will take place with Israel until attacks being carried out by the occupying regime against the Arab country stop.
  • Hezbollah chief: Israeli plots will never materialize; Lebanon neither weak nor under guardianship Press TV 04 May 2026 -- The Hezbollah secretary general says the Israeli regime's plots will never materialize as the resistance movement is forcefully fighting them off, dismissing illusions that Lebanon is a weak county and under the guardianship of others.
  • Hezbollah hits Israeli targets in 11 drone, missile operations Press TV 04 May 2026 -- The Lebanese resistance movement Hezbollah says it has carried out 11 operations against Israeli targets, utilizing drones, missiles, and artillery in a series of coordinated attacks.

  • Middle East: Fighting and displacement in Lebanon; call for 'sustained access' in Gaza UN News 04 May 2026 -- Lebanon's fragile ceasefire is being tested by renewed violence and rising humanitarian needs amid continuing civilians displacement, services under pressure and aid cuts.
  • Peacekeepers step up support to people displaced from south UNIFIL 04 May 2026 -- Over the past week, UNIFIL peacekeepers have ramped up support to the people displaced from the mission's area of operations, especially those forced from towns and villages close to the Blue Line due to ongoing hostilities.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Lebanon, Occupied Palestinian Territory, Ukraine, Sudan UNOCHA 04 May 2026 -- Despite ceasefire, civilians remain under fire

  • Joint call by the President of the ICRC, the Director-General of WHO and the International President of MSF WHO 04 May 2026 -- Ten years ago, the UN Security Council unanimously adopted Resolution 2286 on health care in armed conflicts. The situation is even worse compared to 10 years ago. Today, we mark not an achievement - we mark a failure.

  • Exclusive: China Coast Guard officers raise national flag at Tiexian Jiao in South China Sea, clean up garbage left by illegal Philippine personnel Global Times 04 May 2026 -- The China Coast Guard (CCG) carried out maritime law enforcement and exercised territorial jurisdiction by landing on Tiexian Jiao in the South China Sea on Sunday. The operation came after five Philippine crew members illegally set foot on China's Tiexian Jiao of the Nansha Qundao the same day, the Global Times has learned from the CCG.

  • Truce breach: Heavy clashes erupt in southern Syria; no casualties Press TV 04 May 2026 -- Fierce fighting has erupted between Syrian internal security forces and the so-called National Guard units in the western countryside of Sweida, in yet another violation of the truce.
  • UN winds down Türkiye-Syria aid route, after more than 65,000 lifesaving journeys UN News 04 May 2026 -- The UN has formally concluded its cross-border humanitarian operations from Türkiye into Syria, marking the end of an 11-year mission that served as one of the most complex supply chains in the organization's history.

  • Statement by UN experts deploring widespread attacks on healthcare in Sudan, 10 years after Security Council Resolution 2286 OHCHR 04 May 2026 -- Ten years after the United Nations Security Council unanimously adopted Resolution 2286 condemning attacks on medical facilities and personnel, violations affecting healthcare in Sudan remain widespread, systematic and devastating.

  • Army backs Defense chief in junking peace talks with Reds Philippine News Agency 04 May 2026 -- The Philippine Army (PA) on Monday expressed support to Department of National Defense (DND) Secretary Gilberto Teodoro Jr.'s refusal to resume peace negotiations with the New People's Army (NPA).

News Reports

  • Member of Prolific Russian Ransomware Group Sentenced to Prison US Dept. of Justice 04 May 2026 -- A Latvian national was sentenced today to 102 months in prison for his role in a major Russian ransomware organization that stole from and extorted over 54 companies.

  • Global rise of cyberattacks exposes limits of technical solutions UN News 04 May 2026 -- The internet reaches into every corner of the world and is vital to everything from health systems and financial markets to public services and election organising. This intense global interconnectedness clearly comes with great benefits, but a cybercrime epidemic is putting millions of lives at risk.

  • Meeting with Head of Mordovia Artem Zdunov President of Russia 04 May 2026 -- Vladimir Putin held a working meeting with Head of the Republic of Mordovia Artem Zdunov.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson's Remarks on Bangladeshi Foreign Minister Khalilur Rahman's Visit to China PRC MofA 04 May 2026

  • Lai visits Taiwan-funded projects in Eswatini, urges more private investment CNA 04 May 2026 -- President Lai Ching-te (賴清德) on Sunday visited several projects in Eswatini that are funded by Taiwan, and he called for increased private Taiwanese investment in the country's sole African ally.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 04 May 2026

  • War And Internet Blackout Push Iran's Economy Even Further Toward The Brink RFE/RL 04 May 2026 -- For years, Iran's economy has struggled as international sanctions and administrative mismanagement took their toll.
  • Mossad-linked rioters convicted of murdering security forces executed in northeastern Iran Press TV 04 May 2026 -- Three men convicted of involvement in violent riots, deadly attacks on security forces, and affiliation with the Israeli Mossad spy agency have been executed in Iran's northeastern province of Khorasan Razavi.

  • FM Sa'ar speaks Minister of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation of South Sudan James Pitia Morgan Israel MFA 04 May 2026 -- Minister Morgan updated FM Sa'ar on the situation in his country, and FM Sa'ar briefed Minister Morgan on developments in our region.

  • Oman - Meeting between Jean-Noël Barrot and Sayyid badr Albusaidi, minister of foreign Affairs of the sultanate of oman France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 04 May 2026

  • Opening remarks - Parliament House Prime Minister of Australia 04 May 2026
  • Media statements - Parliament House, Canberra Prime Minister of Australia 04 May 2026
  • Cyber partnership with Japan Prime Minister of Australia 04 May 2026 -- Australia and Japan have agreed to establish a new partnership that will enable deeper cooperation on complex cyber security challenges.

  • Joint Press Statement H.E. Shinjiro Koizumi, Minister of Defense of Japan and H.E. Sjafrie Sjamsoeddin, Minister of Defence of the Republic of Indonesia Jakarta, Indonesia, 4 May 2026 Ministry of Defense of Japan 04 May 2026 -- On 4 May 2026, Minister of Defence Sjafrie of the Republic of Indonesia, and Minister of Defense Koizumi of Japan, held a defense ministers' meeting in Jakarta.
  • Japan-Australia Leaders' Meeting and Signing Ceremony Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 04 May 2026

  • A strategic anchor in southern Europe - Germany is investing in the partnership with Greece Germany Federal Foreign Office 04 May 2026 -- Be it in NATO or the EU, Germany and Greece act in concert. Foreign Minister Wadephul's visit to Athens reflects our close partnership.
  • Foreign Minister Wadephul prior to his departure for Athens Germany Federal Foreign Office 04 May 2026

  • Prime Minister Carney strengthens trade and security partnerships with European nations Prime Minister of Canada 04 May 2026
  • Prime Minister Carney meets with President of France Emmanuel Macron Prime Minister of Canada 04 May 2026
  • Prime Minister Carney meets with Prime Minister of Italy Giorgia Meloni Prime Minister of Canada 04 May 2026
  • Prime Minister Carney meets with Prime Minister of Spain Pedro Sánchez Prime Minister of Canada 04 May 2026
  • Prime Minister Carney meets with President of the European Council António Costa and President of the European Commission Ursula von der Leyen Prime Minister of Canada 04 May 2026

  • PM meeting with Prime Minister Pashinyan of Armenia: 4 May 2026 Gov.UK 04 May 2026 -- The Prime Minister met Prime Minister of Armenia Nikol Pashinyan in Yerevan this afternoon.
  • PM meeting with President of France, Emmanuel Macron and NATO Secretary General, Mark Rutte: 4 May 2026 Gov.UK 04 May 2026 -- The Prime Minister met the President of France, Emmanuel Macron, and NATO Secretary General, Mark Rutte, at the European Political Community Summit earlier today.
  • PM meeting with leaders from Ukraine, France, Italy, Poland, Canada, Norway, Finland, NATO, the European Commission and the European Council at the EPC: 4 May 2026 Gov.UK 04 May 2026 -- The Prime Minister joined leaders from Ukraine, France, Italy, Poland, Canada, Norway, Finland, NATO, the European Commission and the European Council for a meeting on Ukraine this morning.

  • Armenia Gets Time In The Spotlight As European Leaders Gather In Yerevan RFE/RL 04 May 2026 -- The Armenian capital, Yerevan, will get a rare opportunity to be the center of global attention when dozens of world leaders -- and the NATO chief -- gather in the south Caucasus nation for two European-led summits beginning this week.

  • "Türkiye stands out as the most stable country in its region" Presidency of The Republic of Turkey 04 May 2026 -- Delivering remarks following the Presidential Cabinet Meeting, President Erdoğan said: "Every incident we experience further reveals Türkiye's resilience. Every crisis in our region demonstrates the significant distance Türkiye has covered over the past 23 years, further consolidating our country's status as an island of stability. In the current period when paradigms are shifting, uncertainties are increasing, and humanity's search for direction is accelerating, Türkiye distinguishes itself and stands out as the most powerful and most stable country in its region."

  • European Political Community meeting: Press remarks by High Representative Kaja Kallas upon arrival European External Action Service (EEAS) 04 May 2026
  • Remarks by Commissioner Dombrovskis at Eurogroup press conference European Commission 04 May 2026
  • Joint statement following a meeting between President of the European Commission, Ursula von der Leyen, and UK Prime Minister, Keir Starmer European Commission 04 May 2026

  • Joint Statement by the Leaders of France, Lithuania, Estonia, Norway, Poland, the United Kingdom, The President of the European Council, and the High Representative of the Union for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy. France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 04 May 2026

  • 7 OPEC+ countries to increase oil production in June People's Daily 04 May 2026 -- OPEC said in a communique on Sunday that seven OPEC+ countries decided to increase their oil production cap by 188,000 barrels per day in June, in response to the withdrawal of the United Arab Emirates from the bloc.

  • Washington Steps Into Europe's Energy Gap In The Balkans RFE/RL 04 May 2026 -- Since US President Donald Trump took office last year, Washington and Brussels have clashed over trade, support for Ukraine, and military spending. Now there's a new battleground: the Western Balkans.

  • World News in Brief: Iranian Nobel winner's declining health, rising attacks on healthcare, debt crisis hits women, Aid to Mozambique UN News 04 May 2026 -- Concern is mounting over the health of imprisoned Iranian Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi, who was reportedly being held alongside high-risk offenders.
